description: Add a reusable remote workspace bootstrap subsystem for Hal factory runs, covering request/result contracts, idempotent repository and branch setup, tooling and Hal setup checks, safe environment injection, failure classification, sanitized timeline events, and deterministic unit tests with fake executor dependencies.

userStories.title: Define Bootstrap Request And Result Types
userStories.description: As a factory executor developer, I want structured bootstrap input and output types so that future executors can call bootstrap logic without depending on CLI-specific state
acceptanceCriteria: A bootstrap request type captures repository URL, base branch, run branch, workspace directory, required environment keys, and bootstrap options
A bootstrap result type captures final repo path, checked-out branch, executed steps, sanitized timeline events, and failure classification when applicable
Types live in a focused factory bootstrap package or equivalent internal factory subpackage
Types do not reference queue, trigger, verify, policy, secrets, or handoff implementation details
Unit tests assert JSON or struct field behavior for exported machine-readable bootstrap result fields
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define Bootstrap Failure Classifications
userStories.description: As a factory run reporter, I want bootstrap failures classified consistently so that users and automation can identify whether setup failed because of repository access, authentication, dependencies, or engine setup
acceptanceCriteria: Failure categories include repo, auth, dependency, engine_setup, and unknown
Unknown or uncategorized command failures are represented explicitly instead of being mapped to repo, auth, dependency, or engine_setup
Failure classification includes a sanitized message suitable for timeline output
Unit tests cover classification for git clone or fetch errors, authentication failures, missing CLI dependencies, Hal setup failures, and unknown command failures
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Executor Boundary For Bootstrap Commands
userStories.description: As a bootstrap subsystem maintainer, I want command execution behind a small interface so that bootstrap behavior can be tested without invoking real git, Hal, or engine CLIs
acceptanceCriteria: Bootstrap logic depends on an injected executor interface for running commands
The executor interface captures command name, arguments, working directory, environment values, and sanitized output or result metadata
Fake executor tests can assert command ordering, arguments, working directories, and environment values without invoking real CLIs
Production adapter types can be added without changing existing sandbox executor behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bootstrap Repository Checkout
userStories.description: As a factory run, I want bootstrap to clone or update the target repository and check out the requested base branch so that later Hal commands run against the expected codebase state
acceptanceCriteria: When the repository directory does not exist, bootstrap executes or plans a clone from the configured repository URL
When the repository directory already exists, bootstrap executes or plans fetch or update commands instead of recloning
Bootstrap checks out the requested base branch before preparing the run branch
Repository command construction is deterministic and covered by unit tests
Repository failures are classified as repo unless authentication-specific evidence maps them to auth
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Prepare Run Branch Idempotently
userStories.description: As a retried or resumed factory run, I want bootstrap to create or reuse the run branch safely so that repeated bootstrap attempts do not fail when prior setup partially completed
acceptanceCriteria: Bootstrap accepts an explicit run branch name
If the run branch already exists locally, bootstrap reuses it
If the run branch exists remotely or can be fetched, bootstrap checks it out without recreating it
If the run branch does not exist locally or remotely, bootstrap creates it from the requested base branch
Unit tests cover first-run, local retry, and remote branch resume command construction
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify Required Tooling
userStories.description: As a factory run, I want bootstrap to verify required CLIs before execution so that missing dependencies fail early with actionable classification
acceptanceCriteria: Bootstrap verifies Hal is available or installable according to selected bootstrap options
Bootstrap verifies required engine CLIs through configurable command checks
Missing Hal or engine CLIs are classified as dependency when the executable is absent
Hal or engine setup command failures after dependency discovery are classified as engine_setup
Verification commands are recorded in sanitized bootstrap steps
Unit tests cover missing dependency and failed engine setup classification
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refresh Hal Templates And Skills
userStories.description: As a factory run, I want the remote workspace to have current Hal templates, skills, and project standards so that execution behaves like the local repository workflow expects
acceptanceCriteria: Bootstrap includes a step to initialize or refresh .hal templates when needed
Bootstrap includes a step to verify or refresh Hal skills using existing Hal command patterns where available
Bootstrap uses existing template and skill management APIs or commands instead of duplicating ownership logic
Failures during template or skill setup are classified as engine_setup unless a dependency is missing
Unit tests assert the expected setup commands are included in the bootstrap plan
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Inject Required Environment Values Safely
userStories.description: As a factory run, I want required environment values made available to bootstrap commands without printing or storing secrets in plain run events
acceptanceCriteria: Bootstrap request can specify required environment keys and non-secret values needed during bootstrap
Bootstrap command execution receives environment values through the executor boundary
Timeline events and command result records redact configured secret values
Timeline events and command result records redact sensitive environment keys
Serialized timeline events and sanitized command records do not contain secret-looking fixture values in unit tests
This story does not add new policy, secrets storage, or credential handoff behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Record Sanitized Bootstrap Timeline Events
userStories.description: As a factory run observer, I want bootstrap commands and outcomes recorded in timeline-ready events so that setup progress and failures are auditable
acceptanceCriteria: Each bootstrap step produces a timeline-ready event with step name, command summary, status, timestamps or deterministic test equivalents, and sanitized output metadata
Failed step events include failure category and sanitized failure message
Timeline event generation is separate from queue or run persistence
Unit tests assert successful event payloads do not contain plain secrets
Unit tests assert failed event payloads do not contain plain secrets
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Provide High-Level Bootstrap Orchestration
userStories.description: As a future factory executor, I want one reusable bootstrap entrypoint so that executor implementations can prepare workspaces without reimplementing repository, branch, tooling, and Hal setup
acceptanceCriteria: A high-level bootstrap function accepts the bootstrap request type and injected dependencies
The function executes steps in deterministic order: repository setup, base checkout, run branch preparation, tooling verification, Hal template and skill setup, and environment-aware final checks
The function stops on the first blocking failure and returns a classified result
The function is idempotent for repeated calls against an already-prepared fake workspace model
Unit tests cover successful bootstrap, retry or resume bootstrap, and failure stop behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
